question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 124km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 11 units of construction material (78kg each) and 4 units of relief supply box (37kg each). The boat can travel at 48km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 24km is Zone A (speed limit: 35km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 25km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1800kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 10% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 13%.
From 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 14%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 54km waypoint, 7 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 42 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 46 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 46min, 2nd: 54min, 3rd: 62min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

River scout Lee is a veteran who has worked on this river for 8 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 116km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 5 units of relief supply box (64kg each) and 5 units of construction material (52kg each). The boat can travel at 43km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 16km is Zone A (speed limit: 30km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 24km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 14% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 18%.
From 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 14%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 27km waypoint, 2 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 44 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 28 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 7 minutes (1st: 28min, 2nd: 35min, 3rd: 42min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Inland navigator Jung is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 109km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 14 units of medical kit (11kg each) and 4 units of relief supply box (68kg each). The boat can travel at 40km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 3km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 24km is Zone A (speed limit: 31km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 25km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2100kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 14% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 18%.
From 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 15%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 45km waypoint, 2 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 33 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 33min, 2nd: 41min, 3rd: 49min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

From 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 13%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 16km is Zone A (speed limit: 32km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 24km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2500kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 10%.
From 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 11%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 72km waypoint, 7 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 45 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 28 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 28min, 2nd: 36min, 3rd: 44min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 109km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 8 units of relief supply box (69kg each) and 2 units of construction material (56kg each). The boat can travel at 43km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 19km is Zone A (speed limit: 26km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 23km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1500kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 12% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 10%.
From 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 15%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 81km waypoint, 4 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 44 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 43 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 6 minutes (1st: 43min, 2nd: 49min, 3rd: 55min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
resps:
thought
*   Total distance: 109 km
    *   Departure time: 7:00 AM
    *   Boat speed in still water: 43 km/h
    *   River current: 2 km/h
    *   Zone A: First 19 km (Downstream: boat speed + current speed = 43 + 2 = 45 km/h)
    *   Zone B: Remaining distance (109 - 19 = 90 km) (Upstream: boat speed - current speed = 43 - 2 = 41 km/h)

    *   Relief supply boxes: 8 units * 69 kg/unit = 552 kg
    *   Construction material: 2 units * 56 kg/unit = 112 kg
    *   Total initial cargo weight: 552 + 112 = 664 kg
    *   Safety weight threshold: 1500 kg
    *   Cargo weight (664 kg) is less than 1500 kg.
    *   Cargo regulation does not apply.

    *   Zone A speed limit: 26 km/h
    *   Zone B speed limit: 23 km/h
    *   Effective speed in Zone A: min(45 km/h, 26 km/h) = 26 km/h
    *   Effective speed in Zone B: min(41 km/h, 23 km/h) = 23 km/h

    *   Congestion Period: 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM
    *   Congestion reduction: 15% (applied to the current speed limit)
    *   Zone A speed limit during congestion: 26 * (1 - 0.15) = 26 * 0.85 = 22.1 km/h
    *   Zone B speed limit during congestion: 23 * (1 - 0.15) = 23 * 0.85 = 19.55 km/h

    *   Max continuous operation time: 44 minutes
    *   Rest points: Every 9 km (9, 18, 27, 36, 45, 54, 63, 72, 81, 90, 99, 108 km)
    *   Base rest duration: 43 minutes
    *   Fatigue increase: 6 minutes per subsequent rest (1st: 43, 2nd: 49, 3rd: 55, 4th: 61, etc.)

    *   Distance: 19 km
    *   Speed limit: 26 km/h

question:
River engineer Hwang is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 104km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 5 units of construction material (56kg each) and 6 units of water barrel (20kg each). The boat can travel at 42km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 25km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 25km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1700kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 11% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 16%.
From 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 11%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 90km waypoint, 2 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 38 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 7 minutes (1st: 38min, 2nd: 45min, 3rd: 52min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 11 units of relief supply box (39kg each) and 6 units of medical kit (12kg each). The boat can travel at 41km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 22km is Zone A (speed limit: 32km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 23km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2300kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 14% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 11%.
From 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 17%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 63km waypoint, 6 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 35 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 6 minutes (1st: 35min, 2nd: 41min, 3rd: 47min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
resps:
thought
*   Total distance: 105 km
    *   Departure time: 7:00 AM
    *   Cargo:
        *   11 relief supply boxes (39 kg each) = 11 * 39 = 429 kg
        *   6 medical kits (12 kg each) = 6 * 12 = 72 kg
        *   Total initial cargo weight = 429 + 72 = 501 kg
    *   Boat speed (still water): 41 km/h
    *   Current speed: 2 km/h
    *   Zone A: First 22 km (current is downstream, so effective speed = boat speed + current speed)
    *   Zone B: Remaining 83 km (105 - 22 = 83 km) (current is upstream, so effective speed = boat speed - current speed)
    *   Speed limits:
        *   Zone A: 32 km/h
        *   Zone B: 23 km/h
    *   Cargo weight threshold: 2300 kg (If cargo > 2300 kg, speed limits are reduced: Zone A by 14%, Zone B by 11%)
    *   Congestion period: 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM (speed limits reduced by 17%)
    *   Unloading point: 63 km waypoint (6 relief supply boxes unloaded)
    *   Rest rules:
        *   Max continuous operation: 43 minutes
        *   Rest points: Every 9 km (9, 18, 27, 36, 45, 54, 63, 72, 81, 90, 99 km)
        *   Rest duration: 1st = 35 min, 2nd = 41 min, 3rd = 47 min, etc. (increases by 6 min each time)

    *   Initial cargo: 501 kg.
    *   Is 501 kg > 2300 kg? No.
    *   So, no cargo-related speed limit reduction initially.
    *   Zone A speed limit: 32 km/h.
    *   Zone B speed limit: 23 km/h.

    *   Boat speed (still water) = 41 km/h.
    *   Current speed = 2 km/h.
    *   Effective speed in Zone A (downstream): 41 + 2 = 43 km/h.
    *   Effective speed in Zone B (upstream): 41 - 2 = 39 km/h.
    *   Actual speed in Zone A = min(43, 32) = 32 km/h.
    *   Actual speed in Zone B = min(39, 23) = 23 km/h.

    *   **Segment 1: 0 to 9 km (Zone A)**
        *   Speed: 32 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 km / 32 km/h = 0.28125 hours = 16.875 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 16.875 minutes.
        *   Cumulative distance: 9 km.
        *   Continuous operation time: 16.875 minutes. (Less than 43 min, so no mandatory rest yet, but rest is only at 9km intervals).

    *   **Segment 2: 9 to 18 km (Zone A)**
        *   Speed: 32 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 km / 32 km/h = 16.875 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 16.875 + 16.875 = 33.75 minutes.
        *   Cumulative distance: 18 km.
        *   Continuous operation time: 33.75 minutes. (Less than 43 min).

    *   **Segment 3: 18 to 27 km (Zone A then Zone B)**
        *   Zone A ends at 22 km.
        *   Distance in Zone A: 22 - 18 = 4 km.
        *   Time in Zone A: 4 km / 32 km/h = 0.125 hours = 7.5 minutes.
        *   Distance in Zone B: 27 - 22 = 5 km.
        *   Time in Zone B: 5 km / 23 km/h = 5/23 hours ≈ 0.21739 hours = 13.043478 minutes.
        *   Total time for Segment 3: 7.5 + 13.043478 = 20.543478 minutes.
        *   Cumulative operation time since last rest (if any): 33.75 + 20.543478 = 54.293478 minutes.
        *   Wait! The rule is "The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes."
        *   At 18 km, the continuous operation time was 33.75 minutes.
        *   If we continue to 27 km, we'll reach 54.29 minutes, which exceeds 43 minutes.
        *   Therefore, a rest stop is *mandatory* at 18 km.

    *   **Recalculating with mandatory rest at 18 km:**
        *   0 to 9 km: 16.875 min.
        *   9 to 18 km: 16.875 min.
        *   Continuous operation: 33.75 min. (Can we go further? Let's check 18 to 27 km).
        *   If we go from 18 to 27 km, the total continuous operation time would be 33.75 + 20.54 = 54.29 min. This is > 43 min.
        *   So we must rest at 18 km. (Wait, can we rest at 9 km instead? Let's see. If we rest at 9 km, the next segment is 9 to 18, then 18 to 27. 16.875 + 20.54 = 37.415 min, which is < 43 min. Then 27 to 36... Let's see which is more efficient. But the rule says "cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes," so we just need to ensure we stop at a rest point before the 43-minute mark).

    *   **Let's re-evaluate the rest stops carefully:**
        *   Start: 7:00 AM.
        *   0-9 km: 16.875 min. (Total: 16.875)
        *   9-18 km: 16.875 min. (Total: 33.75)
        *   18-27 km: 20.543 min. (Total: 54.293) - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 18 km.
        *   Rest 1 (at 18 km): 35 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 33.75 + 35 = 68.75 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 68.75 min = 8:08:45 AM.

    *   **Segment 3 (18 to 27 km):**
        *   Distance in Zone A: 22 - 18 = 4 km. Time: 4/32 = 7.5 min.
        *   Distance in Zone B: 27 - 22 = 5 km. Time: 5/23 = 13.043 min.
        *   Total time for Segment 3: 20.543 min.
        *   Continuous operation time: 20.543 min.
        *   Cumulative time: 68.75 + 20.543 = 89.293 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 89.293 min = 8:29:17 AM.

    *   **Segment 4 (27 to 36 km):**
        *   Distance in Zone B: 9 km.
        *   Time: 9/23 = 0.3913 hours = 23.478 min.
        *   Continuous operation time: 20.543 + 23.478 = 44.021 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 27 km.
        *   Rest 2 (at 27 km): 41 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 89.293 + 41 = 130.293 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 130.293 min = 9:10:17 AM.

    *   **Segment 4 (27 to 36 km):**
        *   Distance in Zone B: 9 km.
        *   Time: 9/23 = 23.478 min.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 min.
        *   Cumulative time: 130.293 + 23.478 = 153.771 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 153.771 min = 9:33:46 AM.

    *   **Segment 5 (36 to 45 km):**
        *   Distance in Zone B: 9 km.
        *   Time: 9/23 = 23.478 min.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 36 km.
        *   Rest 3 (at 36 km): 47 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 153.771 + 47 = 200.771 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 200.771 min = 10:20:46 AM.

    *   **Wait! We have entered the congestion period (10:00 AM to 12:00 PM).**
        *   Let's re-calculate the time carefully.
        *   At the start of Segment 5 (at 36 km), the time was 9:33:46 AM.
        *   The distance from 36 to 45 km is 9 km.
        *   Speed in Zone B (no congestion): 23 km/h.
        *   Time to reach 10:00 AM:
            *   10:00 AM is 180 minutes from 7:00 AM.
            *   Current time is 153.771 minutes.
            *   Time remaining until congestion: 180 - 153.771 = 26.229 minutes.
            *   Distance covered in 26.229 minutes at 23 km/h:
                *   Distance = (26.229 / 60) * 23 = 0.43715 * 23 = 10.054 km.
            *   But the distance to the next rest point (45 km) is only 9 km.
            *   Time to reach 45 km: 9/23 = 23.478 minutes.
            *   Cumulative time at 45 km: 153.771 + 23.478 = 177.249 minutes.
            *   177.249 minutes is 9:57:15 AM.
            *   So, we reach 45 km *before* the congestion period starts.

    *   **Continuing from 45 km:**
        *   Continuous operation time at 45 km: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956 min. - *Wait, I already said this exceeds 43 min!*
        *   Let's re-examine:
            *   Rest 2 was at 27 km.
            *   27 to 36 km: 23.478 min.
            *   36 to 45 km: 23.478 min.
            *   Total: 46.956 min.
            *   So we MUST rest at 36 km. (I already did this in the previous step).

    *   **Let's re-calculate from Rest 3 (at 36 km):**
        *   Time at 36 km: 153.771 min.
        *   Rest 3 duration: 47 min.
        *   Time after Rest 3: 153.771 + 47 = 200.771 min.
        *   Current time: 10:20:46 AM. (Congestion period has started!)

    *   **Congestion Period Speed Limits (10:00 AM to 12:00 PM):**
        *   Zone A: 32 * (1 - 0.17) = 32 * 0.83 = 26.56 km/h.
        *   Zone B: 23 * (1 - 0.17) = 23 * 0.83 = 19.09 km/h.
        *   Boat's effective speed in Zone B: 39 km/h.
        *   Actual speed in Zone B during congestion: min(39, 19.09) = 19.09 km/h.

    *   **Segment 5 (36 to 45 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 19.09 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 19.09 = 0.47145 hours = 28.287 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 28.287 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 200.771 + 28.287 = 229.058 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 229.058 min = 10:49:03 AM.

    *   **Segment 6 (45 to 54 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 19.09 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 19.09 = 28.287 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 28.287 + 28.287 = 56.574 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 45 km.
        *   Rest 4 (at 45 km): 47 + 6 = 53 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 229.058 + 53 = 282.058 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 282.058 min = 11:42:03 AM.

    *   **Segment 6 (45 to 54 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 19.09 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 19.09 = 28.287 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 28.287 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 282.058 + 28.287 = 310.345 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 310.345 min = 12:10:21 PM.

    *   **Wait! The congestion period ends at 12:00 PM.**
        *   Let's re-calculate Segment 6 (45 to 54 km) more carefully.
        *   At the start of Segment 6 (at 45 km), the time was 11:42:03 AM (282.058 minutes).
        *   Time remaining until congestion ends: 12:00 PM - 11:42:03 AM = 17 minutes 57 seconds = 17.95 minutes.
        *   Distance covered in 17.95 minutes at 19.09 km/h:
            *   Distance = (17.95 / 60) * 19.09 = 0.29917 * 19.09 = 5.711 km.
        *   Remaining distance to 54 km: 9 - 5.711 = 3.289 km.
        *   Speed after 12:00 PM: 23 km/h.
        *   Time to cover remaining distance: 3.289 / 23 = 0.143 hours = 8.58 minutes.
        *   Total time for Segment 6: 17.95 + 8.58 = 26.53 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 26.53 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 282.058 + 26.53 = 308.588 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 308.588 min = 12:08:35 PM.

    *   **Segment 7 (54 to 63 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 26.53 + 23.478 = 50.008 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 54 km.
        *   Rest 5 (at 54 km): 53 + 6 = 59 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 308.588 + 59 = 367.588 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 367.588 min = 1:07:35 PM.

    *   **Segment 7 (54 to 63 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 367.588 + 23.478 = 391.066 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 391.066 min = 1:31:04 PM.

    *   **At 63 km waypoint:**
        *   6 relief supply boxes are unloaded.
        *   Remaining relief supply boxes: 11 - 6 = 5.
        *   Remaining cargo weight: (5 * 39) + (6 * 12) = 195 + 72 = 267 kg.
        *   Is 267 kg > 2300 kg? No.
        *   So, the speed limits remain the same (Zone B: 23 km/h).

    *   **Segment 8 (63 to 72 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 63 km.
        *   Rest 6 (at 63 km): 59 + 6 = 65 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 391.066 + 65 = 456.066 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 456.066 min = 2:36:04 PM.

    *   **Segment 8 (63 to 72 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 456.066 + 23.478 = 479.544 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 479.544 min = 2:59:33 PM.

    *   **Segment 9 (72 to 81 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 72 km.
        *   Rest 7 (at 72 km): 65 + 6 = 71 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 479.544 + 71 = 550.544 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 550.544 min = 4:10:33 PM.

    *   **Segment 9 (72 to 81 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 550.544 + 23.478 = 574.022 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 574.022 min = 4:34:01 PM.

    *   **Segment 10 (81 to 90 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 81 km.
        *   Rest 8 (at 81 km): 71 + 6 = 77 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 574.022 + 77 = 651.022 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 651.022 min = 5:51:01 PM.

    *   **Segment 10 (81 to 90 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 651.022 + 23.478 = 674.5 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 674.5 min = 6:14:30 PM.

    *   **Segment 11 (90 to 99 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956 min. - *Exceeds 43 min!*
        *   So, we must rest at 90 km.
        *   Rest 9 (at 90 km): 77 + 6 = 83 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 674.5 + 83 = 757.5 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 757.5 min = 7:37:30 PM.

    *   **Segment 11 (90 to 99 km):**
        *   Distance: 9 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 9 / 23 = 23.478 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 minutes.
        *   Cumulative time: 757.5 + 23.478 = 780.978 minutes.
        *   Current time: 7:00 AM + 780.978 min = 8:00:59 PM.

    *   **Segment 12 (99 to 105 km):**
        *   Distance: 105 - 99 = 6 km.
        *   Speed: 23 km/h.
        *   Time = 6 / 23 = 0.26087 hours = 15.652 minutes.
        *   Continuous operation time: 23.478 + 15.652 = 39.13 minutes.
        *   39.13 minutes is less than 43 minutes, so no more rests are needed.
        *   Cumulative time: 780.978 + 15.652 = 796.63 minutes.

    *   **Wait, let's double-check all the calculations.**

    *   **Zone A (0-22 km):**
        *   Speed = 32 km/h.
        *   Time = 22 / 32 = 0.6875 hours = 41.25 minutes.
    *   **Zone B (22-105 km):**
        *   Total distance in Zone B = 83 km.
        *   Speed (no congestion) = 23 km/h.
        *   Speed (congestion) = 19.09 km/h.
        *   Congestion period: 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM.
        *   Let's re-calculate the time when the boat enters Zone B:
            *   Departure: 7:00 AM.
            *   Time to reach 22 km:
                *   0-9 km: 16.875 min.
                *   9-18 km: 16.875 min.
                *   (At 18 km, continuous operation = 33.75 min. If we go to 27 km, total = 33.75 + 7.5 (Zone A) + 13.04 (Zone B) = 54.29 min. So we MUST rest at 18 km).
                *   Rest 1 (18 km): 35 min.
                *   18-22 km (Zone A): 4 / 32 = 7.5 min.
                *   Total time to reach 22 km: 16.875 + 16.875 + 35 + 7.5 = 76.25 minutes.
                *   Time: 8:16:15 AM.

    *   **Zone B (22-105 km):**
        *   22 to 27 km: 5 / 23 = 13.043 min. (Continuous: 7.5 + 13.043 = 20.543)
        *   27 to 36 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 20.543 + 23.478 = 44.021) - *Must rest at 27 km.*
        *   Rest 2 (27 km): 41 min.
        *   27 to 36 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478)
        *   36 to 45 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956) - *Must rest at 36 km.*
        *   Rest 3 (36 km): 47 min.
        *   *Current Time check:*
            *   Time to reach 27 km: 76.25 + 13.043 = 89.293 min.
            *   Time after Rest 2: 89.293 + 41 = 130.293 min.
            *   Time to reach 36 km: 130.293 + 23.478 = 153.771 min.
            *   Time after Rest 3: 153.771 + 47 = 200.771 min. (10:20:46 AM)

        *   **Congestion period (10:00 AM to 12:00 PM):**
            *   Speed = 19.09 km/h.
            *   36 to 45 km: 9 / 19.09 = 28.287 min. (Continuous: 28.287)
            *   45 to 54 km: 9 / 19.09 = 28.287 min. (Continuous: 28.287 + 28.287 = 56.574) - *Must rest at 45 km.*
            *   Rest 4 (45 km): 53 min.
            *   *Current Time check:*
                *   Time to reach 45 km: 200.771 + 28.287 = 229.058 min.
                *   Time after Rest 4: 229.058 + 53 = 282.058 min. (11:42:03 AM)

            *   45 to 54 km:
                *   Time until 12:00 PM: 180 - 282.058 = -102.058 (Wait, 12:00 PM is 300 minutes from 7:00 AM).
                *   Time until 12:00 PM: 300 - 282.058 = 17.942 min.
                *   Distance covered in 17.942 min: (17.942 / 60) * 19.09 = 5.708 km.
                *   Remaining distance to 54 km: 9 - 5.708 = 3.292 km.
                *   Time to cover remaining distance: 3.292 / 23 = 0.1431 hours = 8.588 min.

        *   **Post-congestion (12:00 PM onwards):**
            *   Speed = 23 km/h.
            *   54 to 63 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 26.53 + 23.478 = 50.008) - *Must rest at 54 km.*
            *   Rest 5 (54 km): 59 min.
            *   54 to 63 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478)
            *   63 to 72 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956) - *Must rest at 63 km.*
            *   Rest 6 (63 km): 65 min.
            *   63 to 72 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478)
            *   72 to 81 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956) - *Must rest at 72 km.*
            *   Rest 7 (72 km): 71 min.
            *   72 to 81 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478)
            *   81 to 90 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956) - *Must rest at 81 km.*

            *   81 to 90 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478)
            *   90 to 99 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478 + 23.478 = 46.956) - *Must rest at 90 km.*
            *   Rest 9 (90 km): 83 min.
            *   90 to 99 km: 9 / 23 = 23.478 min. (Continuous: 23.478)
            *   99 to 105 km: 6 / 23 = 15.652 min. (Continuous: 23.478 + 15.652 = 39.13) - *No more rests needed.*

    *   **Summing up the total time:**

    *   **Total Time = 33.75 + 35 + 20.543 + 41 + 23.478 + 47 + 28.287 + 53 + 26.53 + 59 + 23.478 + 65 + 23.478 + 71 + 23.478 + 77 + 23.478 + 83 + 39.13**

    *   **Let's add them carefully:**

The first 20km is Zone A (speed limit: 35km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 25km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 14%.
From 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 13%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 27km waypoint, 3 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 42 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 36 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 36min, 2nd: 44min, 3rd: 52min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

        1227 minutes = 20 hours 27 minutes.To find the total travel time for cargo transporter Park, we will break down the journey into segments, applying all regulations step by step.

### 1. Cargo and Speed Regulations
- **Initial Cargo Weight:** (9 boxes × 53 kg) + (3 units × 73 kg) = 477 kg + 219 kg = 696 kg.
- **Cargo Weight Threshold:** 2400 kg. Since 696 kg < 2400 kg, there is no speed reduction due to cargo.
- **Boat Speeds:**
  - Zone A (Upstream): Effective speed = 41 km/h - 2 km/h = 39 km/h. Speed limit = 26 km/h. Actual speed = 26 km/h.
  - Zone B (Downstream): Effective speed = 41 km/h + 2 km/h = 43 km/h. Speed limit = 23 km/h. Actual speed = 23 km/h.
- **Congestion Period (12:00 PM - 1:00 PM):** Speed limits are reduced by 17%.
  - Zone B congestion speed = 23 km/h × (1 - 0.17) = 19.09 km/h.

### 2. Journey Breakdown
The boat must stop at designated rest points (every 9 km) and cannot operate for more than 44 minutes continuously.

- **Leg 1 (0 to 18 km):**
  - Zone A (16 km): $16 / 26 \times 60 \approx 36.92$ min.
  - Zone B (2 km): $2 / 23 \times 60 \approx 5.22$ min.
  - Total Leg 1 time: $36.92 + 5.22 = 42.14$ min. (Within 44 min limit).
  - **Rest 1:** 46 min.
- **Leg 2 (18 to 27 km):**
  - Zone B (9 km): $9 / 23 \times 60 \approx 23.48$ min. (Within 44 min limit).
  - **Rest 2:** 54 min.
- **Leg 3 (27 to 36 km):**
  - Departure time: 9:00 AM + 42.14 + 46 + 23.48 + 54 = 11:45:37 AM.
  - Time until congestion (12:00 PM): $180 - 165.62 = 14.38$ min.
  - Distance traveled before 12:00 PM: $(14.38 / 60) \times 23 \approx 5.51$ km.
  - Remaining distance in Leg 3: $9 - 5.51 = 3.49$ km.
  - Time at congestion speed: $(3.49 / 19.09) \times 60 \approx 10.96$ min.
  - Total Leg 3 time: $14.38 + 10.96 = 25.34$ min. (Within 44 min limit).
  - **Rest 3:** 62 min.
- **Leg 4 to Leg 11 (36 to 108 km):**
  - Each leg is 9 km in Zone B at 23 km/h: $9 / 23 \times 60 \approx 23.48$ min.
  - Total Leg time (8 legs): $8 \times 23.48 = 187.84$ min.
  - Total Rest time (Rests 4-11): $70 + 78 + 86 + 94 + 102 + 110 + 118 + 126 = 784$ min.
- **Leg 12 (108 to 109 km):**
  - Zone B (1 km): $1 / 23 \times 60 \approx 2.61$ min.

- **Sum of Leg Times:** $42.14 + 23.48 + 25.34 + 187.84 + 2.61 = 281.41$ min.
- **Sum of Rest Times:** $46 + 54 + 62 + 784 = 946$ min.
- **Total Travel Time:** $281.41 + 946 = 1227.41$ min.

Rounding to the nearest integer, we get 1227 minutes.

Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 10% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 11%.
From 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 15%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 54km waypoint, 1 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 44 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 44min, 2nd: 52min, 3rd: 60min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Freight captain Han is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 124km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 15 units of construction material (74kg each) and 3 units of relief supply box (30kg each). The boat can travel at 52km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 3km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 25km is Zone A (speed limit: 36km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 23km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2200kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 14%.
From 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 18%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 45km waypoint, 10 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 42 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 29 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 6 minutes (1st: 29min, 2nd: 35min, 3rd: 41min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Answer: 14 hours 30 minutes

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 44 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 39 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 39min, 2nd: 47min, 3rd: 55min, ...).

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2500kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 15% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 17%.
From 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 12%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 90km waypoint, 2 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 44 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 42 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 6 minutes (1st: 42min, 2nd: 48min, 3rd: 54min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

From 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 14%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 90km waypoint, 2 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 44 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 33 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 7 minutes (1st: 33min, 2nd: 40min, 3rd: 47min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

From 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 11%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 27km waypoint, 7 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 43 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 26 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 7 minutes (1st: 26min, 2nd: 33min, 3rd: 40min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

River scout Lee is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 113km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 5 units of construction material (60kg each) and 3 units of water barrel (22kg each). The boat can travel at 48km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 25km is Zone A (speed limit: 32km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 23km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 15%.
From 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 10%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 45km waypoint, 1 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 45 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 44 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 7 minutes (1st: 44min, 2nd: 51min, 3rd: 58min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 13 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 105km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 10 units of water barrel (22kg each) and 5 units of construction material (76kg each). The boat can travel at 43km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 3km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 17km is Zone A (speed limit: 34km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 25km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (2400kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 11% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 14%.
From 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 12%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 36km waypoint, 3 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 42 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 9km). The base rest duration is 42 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 8 minutes (1st: 42min, 2nd: 50min, 3rd: 58min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Channel guide Oh is a veteran who has worked on this river for 7 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 105km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 14 units of construction material (73kg each) and 2 units of relief supply box (58kg each). The boat can travel at 46km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 2km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 33km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 23km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1500kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 14%.
